steps.title: Convert years to model inputs
steps.content: Use years after $1991$ as the input. Then $1991$ becomes $0$, $2011$ becomes $20$, and $2015$ becomes $24$. The two known data points are $(0,57)$ and $(20,224)$, with population measured in thousands.

steps.title: Fit the linear model in Desmos
steps.content: Enter the known points as $x_1=[0,20]$ and $y_1=[57,224]$, then type $y_1\sim m x_1+b$. Desmos returns $m=8.35$ and $b=57$, so the model is $$y=8.35x+57.$$

steps.title: Evaluate at 2015
steps.content: For $2015$, use $x=24$: $$y=8.35(24)+57=257.4.$$ Rounded to the nearest whole number, the estimated population is 257 thousand.
